guest_role: Founder and CEO at Instrumental (ex-Apple Watch product design lead)
assertiveness_why: Harry asks structured, well-prepared multi-part questions with genuine follow-ups (e.g. pressing on how she 'fabricated FOMO'), but never disputes or pressure-tests any of her claims.
question_style: Mostly open-ended prompts that invite frameworks and stories ('talk to me about your methodology'), with follow-up drill-downs on specific details (culture screening, diversity, FOMO mechanics); no interruption or contesting of claims, occasional light joking asides.
tone: Casual and matey, lots of playful banter ('Korean ribs', 'so many good things to dig into here'), enthusiastic and fannish about her Apple pedigree and fundraising story.
depth: Goes past surface bio into tactical operating detail — stack-ranking engineering priorities, structured learning methodology, term-sheet mechanics and pulled term-sheet risk — showing real interest in operational process, not just headline story.
harry_quotes: How did you look to build those relationships?
Were there any mishaps along the way?
learnings: She uses a repeatable five-step framework (identify goal, identify problem, research, define plan, iterate) to rapidly onboard into unfamiliar functional areas like hiring or fundraising.
A public, continuously-updated stack rank of engineering initiatives tied to a single top-line revenue goal keeps the team aligned without top-down micromanagement.
She engineered investor urgency indirectly by scheduling a YC interview and using it as leverage in conversations with VCs she'd already been cultivating, rather than starting from a term sheet.
Founder-hosted dinners were her primary mechanism for building a venture network from zero, since founder referrals carry more weight with VCs than cold outreach.
Even a strong term sheet can be pulled late in diligence, so she advises keeping at least two live high-conviction conversations in parallel rather than shutting down alternatives early.
notable: Episode centers on a genuinely novel fundraising tactic (using a scheduled YC interview to manufacture investor FOMO without an existing term sheet).
